The '''TIE starfighter''' was the first upgraded model of the [[Twin Ion Engine starfighter|T.I.E. starfighter]] produced by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="SWSB">''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]''</ref>

==Characteristics==
A modest upgrade of the [[Twin Ion Engine starfighter|T.I.E. starfighter]] used by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the TIE starfighter had a centralized power generator running both the engine and the weapons, unlike the later [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Line starfighter]], which had separate generators.<ref name="SWSB" /> It was tougher than the final TIE/LN version, but also cost more. It featured weak shields, a small hyperdrive and two one-shot concussion missile launchers, which allowed it to serve as a light bomber, long-assault craft or [[Snubfighter/Legends|snubfighter]]. Unfortunately some of these systems were incompatible with the Twin Ion Engine and required a secondary power supply to operate. The TIE model was slower than the TIE/LN, and its [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] were not as powerful.<ref name="SWAJ10">[[Star Wars Adventure Journal 10|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 10]]</ref>

==History==
The newly formed [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] required a cheap and efficient starfighter that could be mass-produced. [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] especially impressed [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] with TIE starfighters, which cost only a fraction compared to other proposals. The Empire decided to reduce the cost even further by removing secondary energy generators and respective systems, leading to the enormously mass-produced [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]]. The original model still saw some use during the first years of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], but became less common later. By [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the Line model had become the standard TIE fighter in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], and most of the original TIE starfighters were replaced or scrapped.<ref name="TFUCG" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]] features an entry for a previously unmentioned [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]] starfighter called TIE Prototype, which was said to be the predecessor of the standard [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]]. A closer inspection of the entry reveals that this starfighter's description is almost identical to that of the previously established TIE starfighter, and therefore it is simply another designation thereof.<ref name="TFUCG" />
